title: Upload Admission, Untrusted Files, and Quarantine · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs upload admission, untrusted files, and quarantine from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded upload-admission and quarantine workflow after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
Treat filenames as display metadata, normalize safely, reject controls and path confusion, and generate storage keys independently.
Combine bounded signature inspection, declared media type, extension, parser or scanner evidence, and allow-list policy without executing content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 4669 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound upload bytes and concurrency before quarantined publication state; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.

steps.why: Retrieving Upload Accessible Feedback through the concrete bounded upload-admission and quarantine workflow case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Upload Accessible Feedback, keep Upload Accessible Feedback connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Upload Accessible Feedback, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Upload Accessible Feedback precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Upload Accessible Feedback, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 1230 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound upload bytes and concurrency before quarantined publication state; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Upload Accessible Feedback, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: A spinning progress icon is sufficient upload feedback.
